perturbation.original_text: 1.4 “Recipient ’s Team ” means any person who is a member of the Recipient’s team, whether such member is an employee, sub- contractor or agent of the Recipient , or any employee or agent of such person.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 “Recipient ’s Personnel ” means any person who is a member of the Recipient’s team, whether such member is an employee, sub- contractor or agent of the Recipient , or any employee or agent of such person.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Recipient’s Team” to “Recipient’s Personnel” introduces a contradiction because other sections of the document still refer to 'Recipient's Team', creating uncertainty about whether the two terms are interchangeable and creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 The Recipient will hold and keep, and will ensure that all of the Recipient ’s Team will hold and keep, the Confidential Material in confidence and in trust for the City, using at least the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as the Recipient uses to protect its own similar confidential information of like importance, and will, (a) prevent any access, reproduction, disclosure or use of the Confidential Material not expressly authorized herein, (b) disclose the Confidential Material only to those of the Recipient ’s Team who have a definable need to know such information for Purpose, provided that such Recipient ’s Team are bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Recipient no less restrictive than this Agreement, and in the event the employment or appointment of any such person is terminated, the Recipient agrees to use its best efforts to recover any of the Confidential Material in such person’s custody or control. The Recipient will be responsible for all damages arising from any disclosure of all or part of the Co nfidential Material or any act in contravention of this Agreement by a person to whom such Confidential Material was given by the Recipient as if the disclosure were made or the act performed directly by the Recipient , (c) not, and will ensure that each of the Recipient ’s Team will not, copy or reproduce any of the Confidential Material, except as strictly neces sary in order to carry out the Purpose, and (d) promptly notify the City in writing of any unauthorized copying, reproduction, use or disclosure of the Confidential Material of which the Recipient is or becomes aware, and such notice will include a detailed description of the circumstances of the copying, reproduction, use or disclosure and the parties involved.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Recipient’s Team' to 'Recipient’s Personnel' creates an in-text contradiction with section 3.3, where the term 'Recipient’s Team' is still used. This misaligned terminology, as defined in the Perturbation Definitions, introduces uncertainty about whether the two terms are interchangeable and whether the obligations in 3.3 apply to 'Recipient’s Personnel', thus triggering a contradiction. This falls under 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 1.5 “Confidential Material ” means all information, in any form or medium , known or used by City or an Affiliate of the City which is not known to the general public, including, but not limited to, the know -how, trade secrets, strategic plans, technical information, product information, supplier information, customer information, financial information, marketing information and information as to business opportunities, methods and strategies and research and development of the City and its Affiliates. If and to the extent any Confidential Material is included in any report, ass essment, diagram, memorandum or other document or copied or reproduced in any other form or medium, such report, assessment, diagram, memorandum, document or Confidential Material in such other form or medium will be deemed to be Confidential Material.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 “Protected Information ” means all information, in any form or medium , known or used by City or an Affiliate of the City which is not known to the general public, including, but not limited to, the know -how, trade secrets, strategic plans, technical information, product information, supplier information, customer information, financial information, marketing information and information as to business opportunities, methods and strategies and research and development of the City and its Affiliates. If and to the extent any Confidential Material is included in any report, ass essment, diagram, memorandum or other document or copied or reproduced in any other form or medium, such report, assessment, diagram, memorandum, document or Confidential Material in such other form or medium will be deemed to be Confidential Material.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Confidential Material” to “Protected Information” in definition 1.5 but continuing to use “Confidential Material” throughout the rest of that definition and the agreement introduces a contradiction. It is now unclear whether 'Confidential Material' and 'Protected Information' are referring to the same thing and creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.5 “Protected Information ” means all information, in any form or medium , known or used by City or an Affiliate of the City which is not known to the general public, including, but not limited to, the know -how, trade secrets, strategic plans, technical information, product information, supplier information, customer information, financial information, marketing information and information as to business opportunities, methods and strategies and research and development of the City and its Affiliates. If and to the extent any Confidential Material is included in any report, ass essment, diagram, memorandum or other document or copied or reproduced in any other form or medium, such report, assessment, diagram, memorandum, document or Confidential Material in such other form or medium will be deemed to be Confidential Material.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Confidential Material" to "Protected Information" in the definition, while still referencing "Confidential Material" within that same definition and throughout the agreement, creates a strong in-text contradiction. The definition becomes self-referential and unclear. This directly contradicts the requirement for consistent terminology within the contract, as it is now uncertain whether 'Confidential Material' and 'Protected Information' refer to the same thing. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, showing that even within the same clause, the terminology is misaligned after the change.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 All right, title and interest in and to Confidential Material will be and remain vested in the City. Nothing in this Agreement obliges the City to disclose Confidential Material to the Recipient or grants the Recipient any license or right of any kind with respect to Confidential Material, except the limited right to use such information solely for the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 All right, title and interest in and to Sensitive Data will be and remain vested in the City. Nothing in this Agreement obliges the City to disclose Confidential Material to the Recipient or grants the Recipient any license or right of any kind with respect to Confidential Material, except the limited right to use such information solely for the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Confidential Material” to “Sensitive Data” in section 2.1 introduces a contradiction. The text implies that 'Confidential Material' and 'Sensitive Data' are now distinct, leading to uncertainty about what rights and obligations apply to each and creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 All right, title and interest in and to Sensitive Data will be and remain vested in the City. Nothing in this Agreement obliges the City to disclose Confidential Material to the Recipient or grants the Recipient any license or right of any kind with respect to Confidential Material, except the limited right to use such information solely for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies "Confidential Material" to "Sensitive Data" in section 2.1. The contradiction explanation states that this introduces uncertainty because the text implies that 'Confidential Material' and 'Sensitive Data' are now distinct, leading to uncertainty about what rights and obligations apply to each. The contradicted text remains unchanged, still using both terms, thus highlighting the inconsistency introduced by the change in terminology. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as the contract now uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 The Recipient will use Confidential Material onl y as strictly required for the Purpose and only in the manner and upon the terms specified in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 The Recipient will use Confidential Data onl y as strictly required for the Purpose and only in the manner and upon the terms specified in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Confidential Material” to “Confidential Data” in section 3.1, creates an in-text contradiction since “Confidential Material” is a defined term and now there is ambiguity as to what the recipient is obligated to use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 The Recipient will use Confidential Data onl y as strictly required for the Purpose and only in the manner and upon the terms specified in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to "Confidential Material," which is a defined term. The changed text replaces "Confidential Material" with "Confidential Data." The explanation states this introduces ambiguity, as the recipient's obligations are now unclear due to the undefined nature of "Confidential Data" when the original intention was for them to use "Confidential Material." The contradicted text also refers to "Confidential Data", creating a contradiction as to what data is expected to be used, especially since "Confidential Material" has a defined meaning within the original contract, based on the perturbation explanation. This leads to a strong in-text contradiction due to misaligned terminology as defined above.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 All Confidential Material i s provided on an “as is” basis, and all representations and warranties, express or implied, including as to its accuracy or completeness, fitness for purpose, merchantability, and non -infringement, are hereby disclaimed.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 All Sensitive Information i s provided on an “as is” basis, and all representations and warranties, express or implied, including as to its accuracy or completeness, fitness for purpose, merchantability, and non -infringement, are hereby disclaimed.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Confidential Material” to “Sensitive Information” in section 6.1 creates a contradiction. Because Confidential Material is a defined term, but now Sensitive Information is undefined, the reader will not know if “Sensitive Information” falls under the purview of Confidential Material. As such, the agreement will be difficult to enforce as the in-text contradiction has created uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 All Sensitive Information i s provided on an “as is” basis, and all representations and warranties, express or implied, including as to its accuracy or completeness, fitness for purpose, merchantability, and non -infringement, are hereby disclaimed.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Confidential Material" to "Sensitive Information" creates a contradiction. The explanation states that "Confidential Material" is a defined term, while "Sensitive Information" is undefined. The change introduces uncertainty, making the agreement difficult to enforce because the Contradicted Text refers to "Sensitive Information" without it being a defined term. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', which states that in-text contradictions arise when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.
